changelog: **External Correction — Instant Jump mode** (`corrMode = 2`): applies a configurable fraction of the EMA correction vector (0–100 %) as a single mouse report per trigger. Trigger: every received frame (`jumpIntervalMs = 0`) or a user-defined timer (1–20 ms). Gaussian noise (0–50 %) applied independently to X and Y fresh on every jump.

**External Correction — three-way mode selector** in GUI: Classic (fixed α, sample/reaching time), Adaptive (innovation-based α), Jump (instant-jump mode). Alpha and Jump parameter sections show/hide based on selected mode.
**External Correction** — when enabled, the pre-configured MIM velocity profile (VX/VY spline, Gaussian noise) is now fully suppressed; external correction is the sole correction source.
**External Correction** — firmware always boots with correction **disabled** regardless of saved EEPROM state; only an explicit GUI toggle activates it.
**External Correction** — GUI checkbox starts unchecked on every launch; connect response no longer overrides it.
**External Correction** — `get_ext_correction` reads runtime state instead of EEPROM, eliminating repeated `[EEPROM]` log entries in the monitor.
**External Correction** — status poll (`get_ext_correction`) is skipped while correction is OFF; monitor shows only heartbeat and parameter changes.
